value.title: Space Fans - 14 March 2002 quietly marked one of the most...
value.content: ## Space Fans's Post. ### **Space Fans**. 14 March 2002 quietly marked one of the most important moments in modern space history. That month, Elon Musk founded SpaceX with a mission to revolutionize rocket technology and reduce the cost of space travel. The company built its first rocket, Falcon 1, with a small team and limited resources. In 2008 Falcon 1 successfully reached orbit, becoming the first privately developed liquid fueled rocket to do so. That success saved the company and opened the door to everything that followed. Today SpaceX launches more rockets each year than any other company on Earth and continues pushing toward deep space missions. And it all started with a risky decision made in 2002. Elon Musk and All the people around...

value.title: SpaceX timeline: Sky's the limit
value.content: # SpaceX timeline: Sky’s the limit. Take off into the cosmos with our SpaceX timeline! Explore the highs and lows of interstellar exploration as we chart SpaceX's journey to the stars. Embark on a journey to the stars with our **SpaceX timeline**, a visual account of the company’s most important historical milestones and world records achieved. We’ll take a look at both the successes and setbacks they’ve experienced along the way and try to answer some of the most rocket fuel-burning questions you may have. Created using Office Timeline, our time-travel capable PowerPoint add-in, this timeline celebrates humanity’s first steps into privately owned interstellar exploration. Let’s have a look at the most important milestones placed on the SpaceX timeline. * **June 3, 2017**: 1st re-flight of a commercial cargo spacecraft. But, as Musk puts it “[…] fate liked us that day” and the **Falcon 1 became the very first privately owned rocket to make it into the Earth’s orbit**.

value.title: Photos of Key Moments in SpaceX History - Business Insider
value.content: # Photos of key moments in SpaceX history, from the scrappy startup days to milestone rocket launches. Onlookers attending take pictures of a clear view of the SpaceX Falcon 9 on February 14, 2026, in Perris, California. More than two decades later, Elon Musk's space company has decisively accomplished the first, and went public on Friday at a historic $1.8 trillion valuation. From the scrappy startup days to fiery launches (and plenty of explosions) and catching a returning rocket in giant mechanical pincers, these photos and videos show SpaceX's rise to IPO juggernaut. It's SpaceX's first rocket model, called the Falcon 1. Musk leans on a Falcon 1 rocket during an interview in 2004. SpaceX developed its first space-bound rocket, the Falcon 1, between 2002 and 2006. SpaceX's first three attempts to launch the Falcon 1 rocket failed. > Elon Musk examining the debris from Falcon 1's first flight in 2006. Elon Musk sits at a computer during the 2008 launch of the Falcon rocket.

value.title: SpaceX | Spacecraft, Rockets, xAI Acquisition, & 2026 IPO - Britannica
value.content: Dragon capsule by SpaceX docking with the International Space Station on May 25, 2012—the first time a commercial spacecraft did so. SpaceX is an American aerospace company founded in 2002 by Elon Musk that helped usher in the era of commercial spaceflight. SpaceX’s first rocket was the Falcon 1, a two-stage liquid-fueled craft designed to send small satellites into Earth orbit. It was the first private company to successfully launch and return a spacecraft from Earth orbit and the first to launch a crewed spacecraft and dock it with the International Space Station (ISS). In 2010, SpaceX first launched its Falcon 9, a bigger craft so named for its use of nine engines, and the following year it broke ground on a launch site for the Falcon Heavy, a craft the company hoped would be the first to break the $1,000-per-pound-to-orbit cost barrier and that might one day be used to transport astronauts into deep space.
